unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Server-only change (server/src/middleware/error-handler.ts + its co-located error-handler.test.ts): server vitest 167 files / 1934 passed + 1 skipped (1935) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green. Client NOT re-run - detect_frontend_changes.py has_frontend_changes=false (no client/** touched). New D17 coverage: 6 NEW tests in error-handler.test.ts - (1) ELOCKED error -> 409 {error:'sdk-sessions.json is locked, retry'}, (2) ELOCKED prefers 409 over the generic 500 fallback, (3) malformed JSON body (real c.req.json() SyntaxError) -> 400 invalid_json, (4) empty JSON body -> 400 invalid_json, (5) non-body SyntaxError (regex) -> stays 500 (over-catch guard), (6) lowercase-'json' parse SyntaxError -> 400 (case-insensitive guard). All exercise the real Hono app.onError path.

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes (Step 3.8, medium + touches_io_boundary) of the HTTP error-response contract boundary - producer = the global errorHandler (status + JSON body); consumers = client fetch wrappers that gate on the status code (markdownFileApi.ts r.status===409) + the invalid_json code (actionsUpload.ts err.code==='invalid_json'). (1) RED-first probe through the REAL app.request()/app.onError path: pre-fix, an ELOCKED throw and a malformed/empty body both returned 500 (4 new tests RED); post-fix 409/400 (11/11 green). (2) Real-producer probe (throwaway Node script, run then deleted): confirmed Hono 4.12.27 c.req.json() throws instanceof SyntaxError with 'JSON' in the message on BOTH malformed and empty bodies - so the mapping is anchored on the actual framework producer, not a mock. (3) Over-catch guard probe: a non-body SyntaxError (regex/Function defect, no 'json' in message) still surfaces as a truthful 500, and a lowercase-'json' variant still maps to 400 (case-insensitive /json/i). Probe 1 found the defect (RED, fixed); probes 2+3 found nothing new -> two consecutive clean probe rounds => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated. Edge not probed: non-Node runtimes (Bun/CF-Workers) JSON-error shapes - webui is Node-only (node-pty/fs deps), not a real target; a live two-process proper-lockfile race - the synthetic {code:'ELOCKED'} exactly matches proper-lockfile's documented error and the live race is already covered by the per-route rule-6 tests (external/tasks/__tests__/routes.test.ts).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate changes SERVER error-mapping LOGIC only (server/src/middleware/error-handler.ts, the global app.onError). No client/** touched (detect_frontend_changes.py has_frontend_changes=false); no route, UI, Vite-config, proxy, WS-origin, or CORS change. The two contracts (ELOCKED->409, malformed-body->400) require a held cross-process lock and a raw malformed HTTP body respectively - neither is meaningfully reproducible from a browser; both are pinned deterministically by vitest through the real Hono onError path. F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification).

surface_verification.justification: Server-only, behavior-preserving error-contract hardening in the global errorHandler (server/src/middleware/error-handler.ts). detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false (no client/** touched) -> F2 browser-verify honestly skipped. Although the HTTP status returned for two error conditions changes (ELOCKED-contention 500->409; malformed/empty request body 500->400), the client already consumed both established contracts (status===409 retry, invalid_json 400) and no NEW UI affordance, route, Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS/WS surface is introduced. Both conditions are non-browser-reproducible (a held cross-process lock; a raw malformed body) and are pinned deterministically by 6 vitest tests through the real Hono app.onError path plus a real-producer Node probe of c.req.json(). F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - the shared errorHandler now maps proper-lockfile ELOCKED to the retryable 409 body the per-route handlers already emit ({error:'sdk-sessions.json is locked, retry'}) and maps an unguarded request-body parse SyntaxError to the established 400 invalid_json contract; per-route handlers still short-circuit first. The F29 (opaque 500 on lock contention for ~8 store-mutating endpoints) and F33 (500 on malformed/empty body for projects POST/PATCH + settings PUT) scenarios no longer reproduce.
behaviors.evidence: error-handler.test.ts: an Error with code 'ELOCKED' -> 409 with the exact body; malformed and empty JSON bodies (thrown by the real c.req.json()) -> 400 invalid_json; verified all server-side JSON.parse in handlers (settings.ts:34/60, upload.ts:101) is try/catch-guarded so the only SyntaxError reaching the global handler is a body-parse failure.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: 6 NEW tests in error-handler.test.ts. RED captured on pre-fix code: the ELOCKED test expected 409 got 500, the malformed-body and empty-body tests expected 400 got 500 (4 failed / 5 passed). GREEN after the fix: 11/11 passed. The over-catch guard tests (non-body SyntaxError->500, lowercase-json->400) were added during external-review hardening and pin the /json/i narrowing.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite (1934/+1 skipped) + build stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed.
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 167 files / 1934 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green. api-contract-sweep (22-entry baseline) still green - the change adds no rows, only corrects previously-uncovered 500 leaks. DO-NOT rule 6 (multi-writer state files use proper-lockfile; PATCH surfaces ELOCKED as 409) preserved and extended. Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact - errorHandler writes nothing. Client not re-run (has_frontend_changes=false).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Footprint exactly the spec's two files: server/src/middleware/error-handler.ts (42 -> 89, well under the 300 source limit, NOT baselined) + server/src/middleware/error-handler.test.ts (64 -> 169, under 300, NOT baselined). No shipwright_bloat_baseline.json entry touched. Serial #17, deps/blocks none, D01-D16 merged -> no live parallel collision.
